Component_Internal_InputSplitter.description: A brick that can split a Player Input wire from a seat into individual inputs, allowing custom control setups.

Character.tooltip: Connect a Player reference to allow people to send inputs to this component.

InputForward.tooltip: The player's movement input on the X axis.

bPressedJump.tooltip: Set to true if the player is pressing their jump key.

InputRight.tooltip: The player's movement input on the Y axis.

Component_Internal_WeightBrick.description: A brick that can be set to a customized weight

Component_Internal_WheelEngine.description: Takes control inputs and uses them to drive & steer vehicle wheels. Great for cars, trucks, tanks, and anything else that drives around on the ground.

ManualInput_Drive.tooltip: Manual amount for the engine to drive. -1 = full reverse, 1 = full accelerate.

CenterOfSteering.tooltip:
How far along the vehicle's steering box (box containing all wheels) the center of steering is.
By default, we put it at the very back of the vehicle (0). Wheels at the front will full-steer, wheels at the back won't steer at all.
If you'd like, you can bring it to the very front of the vehicle (1). Wheels at the back will full-steer, wheels at the front won't steer at all.

bEnabled.tooltip: Whether the engine can ever be on.

Character.tooltip: Connect a Player reference here to allow a player to drive the engine. Seats also provide a Player wire which can be used here.

DriveAcceleratingPowerMultiplier.tooltip:
Multiplier applied to drive joint torque when accelerating.
If above 1.0, the engine will torque your joint harder than the torque you set.

AudioDescriptor.tooltip: The set of sounds the engine uses.

ManualInput_Steer.tooltip: Manual amount for the engine to steer. -1 = full left, 1 = full right.

bTankSteering.tooltip:
If true, the engine attempts to steer by driving the wheels on either side of the vehicle at different speeds (i.e. tank controls)
This means the engine won't ever turn the wheels except to correct heading if they are misaligned.

bManualInput_Brake.tooltip:
Manual braking mode. True means the engine will brake. 
When a vehicle brakes, the speed of all joints is hard-set to 0 and the torque is set to the braking torque.

DriveSpeed.tooltip:
Determines the max speed at which the engine attempts to drive wheels.
Ignored while braking, obviously.

bEnableManualControl.tooltip: If true, the engine always stays on as long as Enabled is true. When not driven by a player, it will be controlled by the manual input values.

DriveInterpSpeed.tooltip:
Determines how fast the wheel target speed goes from 0 to the top speed. Useful to avoid your car tipping backwards when you accelerate from stopped.
Set 1, and your wheels will not be trying to reach the max speed for a full second.
Set 10, and it will take a tenth of a second.
0 makes it instant, if you enjoy having your car fly upwards when accelerating from stopped.

DriveBrakingPowerMultiplier.tooltip:
Multiplier applied to drive joint torque when braking.
If above 1.0 (which it probably should be), the engine will torque your joint harder than the torque you set.

SteerInterpSpeed.tooltip:
Determines how fast your wheels steer to their current target.
Set 1, and your wheels will take a full second to steer to the target.
Set 10, and your wheels will take a tenth of a second.
0 makes it instant.

DriveDampingMultiplier.tooltip:
Multiplier applied to drive joint damping, which is used when neither accelerating nor braking (models the vehicle "rolling" to a stop).
If above 1.0, the engine will dampen your joint harder than the damping you set.

SteerLimitDegree.tooltip: The maximum in both directions that the engine can steer wheels - i.e. the value in degrees of Steer 1.0 (and if negated, the value of steer -1.0)

TankSteerSpeedMultiplier.tooltip: Multiplier applied to the drive speed when tank steering.

Control_DriveAndSteer.tooltip: Connected to wheel joints to allow this engine to drive and steer them.

Component_OneShotAudioEmitter.description: Plays a sound when activated by a wire signal.

RepeatVariance.tooltip: An applied value of randomness to the base repeat time.

RepeatTime.tooltip: The amount of base time until the sound will repeat again.

bEnableRepeat.tooltip: Toggles whether or not the sound will automatically repeat when the play signal is kept on.

PitchMultiplier.tooltip: How much to multiply the default pitch of the audio being played.

VolumeMultiplier.tooltip: How much to multiply the default volume of the audio being played.

MaxDistance.tooltip: The distance the audio can no longer be heard.

bSpatialization.tooltip: Whether or not to spatialize the audio in the game world, panning accordingly as the player moves around. When disabled, it will play as a 2D sound.

InnerRadius.tooltip: The distance before the audio will begin to fade out.

bPlay.tooltip: Plays audio when it first receives an on signal.

AudioDescriptor.tooltip: The audio track to play on this brick.

Component_AudioEmitter.description: Plays audio from a wide selection of music tracks and sound effects.

NonFocusAzimuth.tooltip: Sounds above this angle on the player's screen will be considered out of focus, and will have the Unfocused Volume Multiplier applied to it. You can set this value lower than the Focus Angle to flip the effect.

VolumeMultiplier.tooltip: How much to multiply the default volume of the audio being played.

InnerRadius.tooltip: The distance before the audio will begin to fade out.

AudioDescriptor.tooltip: The audio track to play on this brick.

FocusAzimuth.tooltip: Sounds played from within this angle on the player's screen will be considered in focus, and played normally.

PitchMultiplier.tooltip: How much to multiply the default pitch of the audio being played.

MaxDistance.tooltip: The distance the audio can no longer be heard.

bSpatialization.tooltip: Whether or not to spatialize the audio in the game world, panning accordingly as the player moves around. When disabled, it will play as a 2D sound.

NonFocusVolumeAttenuation.tooltip: When a sound is out of focus, it will have its volume multiplied by this value.

Component_Target.description: Outputs a wire signal when hit with a projectile.

OnTime.tooltip: The amount of time that the target turns "On" when shot. Set to 0 for one tick. The target cannot report new information while it is on.

Damage.tooltip: How much damage came from the hit.

bJustHit.tooltip: Whether the target was just hit and is waiting out its hit timeout.

CharacterThatJustHit.tooltip: Character that just hit the target.

RunSpeed.tooltip: Multiplier for the walking speed of the bot

bTeamCollision.tooltip: Whether a bot  should collide with characters using channel 1.

bTeamCollision1.tooltip: Whether a bot  should collide with characters using channel 2.

Jumpyness.tooltip: How often does the bot jump

bVisionRaycasting.tooltip: Require line of sight to attack someone.

bTeamCollision3.tooltip: Whether a bot  should collide with characters using channel 4.

RespawnTime.tooltip: How long we should wait before spawning a new bot after the current one dies.

bTeamCollision2.tooltip: Whether a bot  should collide with characters using channel 3.

AttackMovementAmount.tooltip: How much does the bot move around while in combat.

ReactionTime.tooltip: How often does the bot recalculate targets.

Agression.tooltip: How much does the bot keep shooting.

HealthMultiplier.tooltip: Multiplies the amount of health of the bot

TeamCollisionChannel.tooltip: Which collision channel the bot should use.

CorpseTimeout.tooltip: How long we should wait before deleting corpses.

AggroRange.tooltip: Minimum distance the bot requires to attack a target.

GunSkill.tooltip: Accuracy of the bot when using guns

MovementRandomness.tooltip: Random offset applied to movement logic

MovementAmount.tooltip: How often the bot refreshes movement.

FocusCharacter.tooltip: Character this bot is trying to attack

DistanceToTarget.tooltip: Distance to the current movement goal

Component_Internal_AnimatedButton.description: Outputs a wire signal when clicked. Remains active so long as the interact button is held down.

ReleaseSound.tooltip: Sound played when you release the button.

PressSound.tooltip: Sound played when you press the button down.

bAllowNearbyInteraction.tooltip: Allows players near the button in third person to interact with this brick without aiming at it.

bHiddenInteraction.tooltip: This interactable will not change the player's crosshair or pop up any labels.

PromptCustomLabel.tooltip: Text label to use when the prompt widget appears.

PressCount.tooltip: Number of people currently holding this button.

Character.tooltip: Character that just interacted with this brick.

Component_CheckPoint.description: When clicked, a player will respawn here if they die.

Component_GoalPoint.description: A brick which can be used as a goal to reach in minigames.

Component_Internal_Seat.description: Take a seat. Used to control vehicles and other things.

bIsOccupied.tooltip: Whether a character is sitting in this seat or not.

SittingCharacter.tooltip: Character that just interacted with this brick.

Component_Internal_AnimatedSwitch.description: A switch that flips when interacted with. Outputs True when turned on, and outputs False when turned off.

InteractSound.tooltip: Sound that plays when interacting with this switch.

bAllowNearbyInteraction.tooltip: Allows players near the button in third person to interact with this brick without aiming at it.

bHiddenInteraction.tooltip: This interactable will not change the player's crosshair or pop up any labels.

PromptCustomLabel.tooltip: Text label to use when the prompt widget appears.

bEnabled.tooltip: Whether the switch is switched on.

Character.tooltip: Character that just interacted with this brick.

Component_Internal_Microchip.description: Allows you to place other logic gates on the microchip's circuitboard to save space. The entire circuitboard collapses down into the microchip when not in use.

Component_Internal_Rerouter.description: A node which wires can be routed through for more direct control over their shape. Useful for organizing cluttered logic circuits.

BrickComponentType_WireGraphPseudo_Var.description: Allows defining a variable, which can be modified and read via other Variable gates.

BrickComponentType_WireGraphPseudo_Array.description: Allows defining an array variable, which can be modified and read via other Array Variable gates.

Arguments.tooltip: The raw argument string passed after the command name.

OutlineSize.tooltip: Font outline thickness in pixels. 0 disables the outline.

OutlineColor.B.tooltip: The blue component of the color.

FontColor.G.tooltip: The green component of the color.

Transition.tooltip: Duration in seconds to interpolate from the previous state to the new state. 0 for instant.

OutlineColor.G.tooltip: The green component of the color.

Lifetime.tooltip: How long the text remains visible in seconds. 0 for infinite.

OutlineColor.A.tooltip: The alpha component of the color.

TextId.tooltip: Override the display text identity. When non-zero, multiple gates sharing the same ID control one piece of text. 0 uses the brick's persistent handle. Connect the Text ID output of another Display Text gate to chain updates to the same text.

Text.tooltip: Rich text content to display. Supports markup like <b>, <i>, <size="24">, <color="FF0000">, <emoji>, and <icon>.

AnchorX.tooltip: Horizontal screen anchor as a percentage (0 = left, 0.5 = center, 1 = right).

OutlineColor.R.tooltip: The red component of the color.

FontColor.R.tooltip: The red component of the color.

FontColor.B.tooltip: The blue component of the color.

FontColor.A.tooltip: The alpha component of the color.

PositionX.tooltip: Horizontal offset from the anchor in slate units.

PositionY.tooltip: Vertical offset from the anchor in slate units.

AnchorY.tooltip: Vertical screen anchor as a percentage (0 = top, 0.5 = center, 1 = bottom).

BrickComponentType_WireGraph_Exec_Entity.displayName: Add Location and Rotation to Entity

Rotation.Yaw.tooltip: The Yaw. If you were rotating a plane on the yaw axis, changing the yaw would spin the plane around!

Rotation.Roll.tooltip: The Roll. If you were rotating a plane on the roll axis, changing the roll would roll it around like a barrel!

Rotation.Pitch.tooltip: The Pitch. If you were rotating a plane on the pitch axis, changing the pitch would point its nose up and down!

BrickComponentType_WireGraph_Exec_Entity.displayName: Get Location and Rotation of Entity

Rotation.Roll.tooltip: The Roll. If you were rotating a plane on the roll axis, changing the roll would roll it around like a barrel!

Rotation.Pitch.tooltip: The Pitch. If you were rotating a plane on the pitch axis, changing the pitch would point its nose up and down!

Rotation.Yaw.tooltip: The Yaw. If you were rotating a plane on the yaw axis, changing the yaw would spin the plane around!

BrickComponentType_WireGraph_Exec_Entity.displayName: Set Location and Rotation of Entity

Rotation.Yaw.tooltip: The Yaw. If you were rotating a plane on the yaw axis, changing the yaw would spin the plane around!

Rotation.Roll.tooltip: The Roll. If you were rotating a plane on the roll axis, changing the roll would roll it around like a barrel!

Rotation.Pitch.tooltip: The Pitch. If you were rotating a plane on the pitch axis, changing the pitch would point its nose up and down!

Rotation.Roll.tooltip: The Roll. If you were rotating a plane on the roll axis, changing the roll would roll it around like a barrel!

Rotation.Pitch.tooltip: The Pitch. If you were rotating a plane on the pitch axis, changing the pitch would point its nose up and down!

Rotation.Yaw.tooltip: The Yaw. If you were rotating a plane on the yaw axis, changing the yaw would spin the plane around!

SpawnOffsetRotation.Pitch.tooltip: The Pitch. If you were rotating a plane on the pitch axis, changing the pitch would point its nose up and down!

SpawnOffsetRotation.Roll.tooltip: The Roll. If you were rotating a plane on the roll axis, changing the roll would roll it around like a barrel!

SpawnOffsetRotation.Yaw.tooltip: The Yaw. If you were rotating a plane on the yaw axis, changing the yaw would spin the plane around!

Blend.tooltip: The alpha value for blending between Input A and Input B. 0 means only Input A, 1 means only Input B. Anything between is a mixture of the two - for example, 0.5 would give you a value half way between A and B.

Input.R.tooltip: The red component of the color.

Input.B.tooltip: The blue component of the color.

Input.A.tooltip: The alpha component of the color.

Input.G.tooltip: The green component of the color.

bCollisionEnabled_Player.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 1.

bCollisionEnabled_Player1.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 2.

bCollisionEnabled_Player2.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 3.

bCollisionEnabled_Player3.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 4.

bCollisionEnabled_Player.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 1.

bCollisionEnabled_Player1.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 2.

bCollisionEnabled_Player2.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 3.

bCollisionEnabled_Player3.tooltip: Enable detection of players in channel 4.

ZeroSecondsToWait.tooltip: How long to hold the output after the input goes to zero. Set to -1 to use Seconds to Delay instead.

ZeroTicksToWait.tooltip: How long to hold the output after the input goes to zero. Set to -1 to use Ticks to Delay instead.

bIsLooseZone.tooltip: Whether this zone is a loose zone, meaning objects don't have to be fully inside it to be considered to be inside.

bIsBuildingZone.tooltip: Whether this zone is a place that people without permission to build outside of build zones can build.

bIsShareZone.tooltip: Whether this zone is a share zone, meaning bricks placed inside that are on the same grid and owned by the same owner are publicly editable by anyone on the server without them needing trust from the owner.

Component_Damage.description: A deadly spike which can damage or kill players on contact. Appears as an array of multiple spikes when stretched on a horizontal axis.

Message.tooltip: If this is set to anything, a player will see that message upon touching the brick.

ConsoleTag.tooltip: If this is set to anything, the string it is set to will be printed to the console.

BrickComponentType_Internal_TeleportDest.description: Serves as a target location for gameplay effects that launch and teleport players/entities.
